2606.19951.title: Investigating Human-Model Discrepancies in Speech Quality Assessment via Acoustic and Prosodic Perturbations
2606.19951.paper_summary: Mean opinion score (MOS) prediction models are widely used as proxy metrics in text-to-speech (TTS) research, yet their ability to capture quality differences beyond acoustic fidelity remains unclear. We investigate this via controlled perturbations on speech: acoustic degradation, prosodic errors, and manipulation of speaker-specific characteristics such as pitch and speaking rate. We obtained MOS predictions for these speech samples from both human listeners and the model, and analyzed the differences in their perceptual characteristics. Results show that most models track acoustic degradation well, while all are insensitive to prosodic errors despite large subjective score drops. For speaker characteristics, models exhibit a double dissociation: strong mean fundamental frequency (F0) biases absent in human ratings, yet insensitivity to speaking rate and F0 variability that humans notice. These findings highlight limitations of scalar MOS prediction beyond acoustic fidelity.
2606.19951.paper_summary_zh: 平均意見分數（MOS）預測模型在文本轉語音（TTS）研究中被廣泛用作代理指標，但它們在捕捉超越聲學真實性的質量差異方面的能力仍不清楚。<lb/>我們通過對語音進行控制擾動來調查這一點：聲學降級、韻律錯誤以及操控說話者特定特徵，如音高和說話速度。<lb/>我們從人類聽眾和模型中獲得了這些語音樣本的MOS預測，並分析了它們在感知特徵上的差異。<lb/>結果顯示，大多數模型能很好地追踪聲學降級，而所有模型對韻律錯誤則不敏感，儘管主觀評分大幅下降。<lb/>在說話者特徵方面，模型表現出雙重解離：強烈的平均基頻（F0）偏差在人工評分中缺失，但對說話速度和F0變異性不敏感，而人類則能察覺到這些。<lb/>這些發現突顯了超越聲學真實性的標量MOS預測的局限性。
2606.19951.author: Masato Takagi et.al.
2606.19951.authors: Masato Takagi, Masaya Kawamura, Reo Shimizu, Yuma Shirahata
